name: tidy
description: Tidy-as-you-touch: when you edit a file, format it and surface linter findings — fixing what's safe — and hold a subtractive posture (reuse before create, delete what a change makes redundant) so an actively-worked project converges toward clean code and trends down in complexity over time, scoped to the file you touched. The standard goes quiet (one-line re-anchor) once it's recorded in the project's CLAUDE.md. The /tidy:distill command runs a read-only whole-project weight report and drives an on-demand prune pass. MVP targets Go (goimports/gofumpt + golangci-lint); other languages no-op gracefully.
